name: com.zacharysnewman.inventory
description: A flexible and configurable inventory system for Unity supporting typed containers (Bomb Bag, Quiver, Wallet, etc.), stackable items, multiple currency types, and currency-gated purchases.

samples.description: Bomb Bags, Quivers, and Wallets in three sizes each; sword and bow equipment slots; collectible heart containers; potion bottles; rupee-gated shop purchases; and bomb-bag upgrade with item transfer.
Primary, secondary, and tertiary weapon slots (tertiary gated behind a Large Backpack pickup); lethal and tactical equipment slots; armor plate storage; and cash-based buy-station purchases.
InventoryRenderer MonoBehaviour with Inspector-wired TMP_Text bindings for item counts, currency amounts, and container slot contents/visibility. Driven entirely by Inventory events — no polling.
Merchant that sells consumables, weapons, and a one-of-a-kind amulet (maxGlobalCount). Demonstrates TryPurchase with PurchaseResult, a sell-back helper, and a buy-max pattern using HowManyCanAdd.
Player backpack paired with a home stash. Demonstrates TryTransferAll (per-item and bulk), TryTransferFrom, Container.Sort, and GetItems with a filter predicate.
Checkpoint save/load modelled on a Dark Souls bonfire. Demonstrates Inventory.Save(), Inventory.Load(), and InventorySnapshot as the data contract for persistence.
